questions.text: Who is the protagonist and skilled bootmaker in the story 'Quality'?
questions.explanation: Mr. Gessler was a German bootmaker living in London, known for his excellent craftsmanship.

questions.text: What was the distinguishing feature of the Gessler Brothers' shop?
options: It was very quiet and had no sign except their name
It was in a crowded mall
questions.explanation: The shop had a quiet, respectful atmosphere, unlike modern commercial stores.

questions.text: According to Mr. Gessler, making boots was a/an:
questions.explanation: He viewed his work with great pride and artistic dedication.

questions.text: The boots made by Gessler were known for their:
questions.explanation: They lasted a very long time and were made from the best leather.

questions.text: Why did the narrator not visit Gessler's shop very often?
questions.explanation: The quality was so good that the boots simply did not wear out quickly.

questions.text: What did Mr. Gessler say about 'Big Firms'?
options: They have no self-respect and use advertisement instead of work
questions.explanation: He was bitter about how big companies took away work through marketing rather than quality.

questions.text: Mr. Gessler had a strong ________ accent.
questions.explanation: His speech reflected his origins, such as saying 'Id is an ard' instead of 'It is an art'.

questions.text: What happened to the elder Gessler brother?
questions.explanation: The loss of his brother deeply affected the younger Mr. Gessler.

questions.text: How did the younger Mr. Gessler die eventually?
questions.explanation: He spent all his money on rent and leather, neglecting his own food to keep the shop going.

questions.text: The narrator felt ________ when he heard about Mr. Gessler's death.
questions.explanation: He admired Gessler's commitment to quality despite the hardships.

questions.text: When the narrator once mentioned a pair of boots creaked, what was Gessler's reaction?
options: He was shocked and offered to fix or refund them
questions.explanation: He took his reputation seriously and couldn't believe his boots could be faulty.

questions.text: The story 'Quality' is written by:
questions.explanation: John Galsworthy is a famous English novelist and playwright.

questions.text: Mr. Gessler's shop was in a fashionable part of ________.
questions.explanation: He lived and worked in the heart of London.

questions.text: Gessler used only ________ leather to make his boots.
questions.explanation: Quality was his priority, so he never compromised on materials.

questions.text: The new owner of the shop told the narrator that Gessler was a:
options: Character who made the best boots in London
questions.explanation: Even the competition acknowledged that Gessler was an unmatched craftsman.

questions.text: What was the main reason for Gessler's business failure?
options: He couldn't compete with advertising and speed of big firms
questions.explanation: His slow, artisanal method couldn't survive in a fast-paced commercial world.

questions.text: The boots made by Gessler 'seemed to have the ________ of boots' in them.
questions.explanation: The narrator felt the boots were more than just footwear; they were a work of art.

questions.text: Mr. Gessler would often look at the leather with ________ eyes.
questions.explanation: He truly appreciated the beauty and quality of the raw materials he used.

questions.text: The narrator ordered many pairs of boots because:
options: He wanted to help the struggling Mr. Gessler
He was going on a trip
questions.explanation: He felt pity for the old man's declining condition and wanted to support him.

questions.text: What does the title 'Quality' signify in the story?
options: The high standards of Gessler's work
questions.explanation: It refers to the uncompromising excellence and integrity of the craftsman.

questions.text: When the narrator entered the shop, he felt like he was entering a ________.
questions.explanation: The quiet and solemn atmosphere gave the shop a sacred feeling.

questions.text: Mr. Gessler's hands were always ________ when he was working.
questions.explanation: His physical state showed the marks of his hard work.

questions.text: The elder brother was often ________ with the younger one.
questions.explanation: The two brothers were very similar in appearance and voice.

questions.text: How many shops did the Gessler brothers have originally?
questions.explanation: The shop consisted of two small shops combined.

questions.text: The 'big firms' had no ________ according to Gessler.
questions.explanation: He believed they cared only about profit and not about the craft.

questions.text: The narrator felt 'heavy-hearted' when he saw Gessler after a long time because:
options: Gessler looked very old and worn out
questions.explanation: Gessler's aging and poverty were visible and saddening.

questions.text: Gessler would not let anyone ________ his boots.
questions.explanation: He was very protective of his work and its quality.

questions.text: What was the final fate of the shop?
options: It was taken over by an Englishman
It was closed by the government
questions.explanation: After Gessler's death, another person took over the premises.

questions.text: The narrator says Gessler made boots 'by ________'.
questions.explanation: Hand-made boots were the hallmark of his quality.

questions.text: Which of these words describes Gessler's life?
questions.explanation: He sacrificed his health and comfort for his art.

questions.text: Gessler's boots 'never failed to fit'. Fit here means:
options: Be the right size/shape for the foot
questions.explanation: The comfort and precision of his work were perfect.

questions.text: The younger brother always called his work a '________'.
questions.explanation: His German accent made 'Art' sound like 'Ard'.

questions.text: What was the narrator's attitude towards the big firms' boots?
options: He bought them in an emergency but didn't like the quality
questions.explanation: He once entered Gessler's shop wearing big-firm boots, which prompted Gessler's bitter remarks.

questions.text: The Gessler brothers were ________ immigrants.
questions.explanation: They brought their German precision and skills to London.

questions.text: How did Gessler feel about the leather he used?
options: He had a 'divine' respect for it
He thought it was too expensive
questions.explanation: He viewed the materials with a reverence that only an artist has.

questions.text: The boots produced by Gessler had 'something of himself' in them. This means:
options: They showed his hard work and character
They had his hair in them
questions.explanation: His dedication was reflected in the final product.

questions.text: Was Gessler a successful businessman in terms of profit?
questions.explanation: He was a great artist but a poor businessman in the modern sense.

questions.text: The story emphasizes the ________ of the craftsman in the industrial age.
questions.explanation: It laments how traditional skills are being lost to mass production.

questions.text: Mr. Gessler spent his last days ________.
questions.explanation: He never gave up on his work, even when he had nothing to eat.

questions.text: The narrator's final tribute to Gessler was: 'He made ________ boots.'
questions.explanation: Simple but powerful, it summarized his life's work.
